svg.text: evaluate_next_zone() / TemperatureMonitor::evaluateNextZone() - one transition per 100 µs sample
rising edge - enters a more critical zone (pinned to the spec'd threshold, 0 offset)
recovery edge - returns to a less critical zone (offset by the full +/-1.0 degC tolerance)
The +/-1 degC tolerance (TEMP_HYSTERESIS_TOLERANCE_DECI_C = 10) is applied identically to all three
boundaries (5.0 / 85.0 / 105.0 degC) and to both directions of every one of them - see the table below.
The rising edge of each boundary is pinned to the exact requirement ("warning >= 85 degC", etc.) and so
carries a 0.0 degC offset by construction; shifting it would either delay a safety-critical alarm or
contradict the requirement's literal threshold. The recovery edge is where a reading sitting on the
boundary would otherwise make the LED flicker, so that is where the full 1.0 degC is subtracted/added.
Both edges of every boundary are therefore derived from the exact same nominal-threshold +/- tolerance
formula - the tolerance is used everywhere, it just algebraically simplifies to 0 on the rising edge.
Every boundary gets the same treatment - nominal threshold, tolerance, rising edge, falling edge
